description: The Llama Nemotron Reranking 1B model is optimized for providing a logit score that represents how relevant a document(s) is to a given query. The model was fine-tuned for multilingual, cross-lingual text question-answering retrieval, with support for long documents (up to 8192 tokens). This model was evaluated on 26 languages: English, Arabic, Bengali, Chinese, Czech, Danish, Dutch, Finnish, French, German, Hebrew, Hindi, Hungarian, Indonesian, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Norwegian, Persian, Polish, Portuguese, Russian, Spanish, Swedish, Thai, and Turkish. This model is a component in a text retrieval system to improve the overall accuracy. A text retrieval system often uses an embedding model (dense) or lexical search (sparse) index to return relevant text passages given the input. A reranking model can be used to rerank the potential candidate into a final order. The reranking model has the question-passage pairs as an input and therefore, can process cross attention between the words. It’s not feasible to apply a Ranking model on all documents in the knowledge base, therefore, ranking models are often deployed in combination with embedding models. This model is ready for commercial use. The Llama Nemotron Reranking 1B model is a part of the NeMo Retriever collection of NIM, which provide state-of-the-art, commercially-ready models and microservices, optimized for the lowest latency and highest throughput. It features a production-ready information retrieval pipeline with enterprise support. The models that form the core of this solution have been trained using responsibly selected, auditable data sources. With multiple pre-trained models available as starting points, developers can also readily customize them for their domain-specific use cases, such as information technology, human resource help assistants, and research & development research assistants. We are excited to announce the open sourcing of this commercial embedding model. For users interested in deploying this model in production environments, it is also available via the model API in NVIDIA Inference Microservices (NIM). The Llama Nemotron Reranking 1B model is most suitable for users who want to improve their multilingual retrieval tasks by reranking a set of candidates for a given question. The Llama Nemotron Reranking 1B model is a transformer cross-encoder fine-tuned with contrastive learning. We employ bi-directional attention when fine-tuning for higher accuracy. The last embedding output by the decoder model is used with a mean pooling strategy, and a binary classification head is fine-tuned for the ranking task. Ranking models for text ranking are typically trained as a cross-encoder for sentence classification. This involves predicting the relevancy of a sentence pair (for example, question and chunked passages). The CrossEntropy loss is used to maximize the likelihood of passages containing information to answer the question and minimize the likelihood for (negative) passages that do not contain information to answer the question. We trained the model on public datasets described in the Dataset and Training section. The model takes a pair of texts as input, formatted as a list of text pairs. The input parameters are one-dimensional. The model was trained on question and answering over text documents from multiple languages. It was evaluated to work successfully with up to a sequence length of 8192 tokens. Longer texts are recommended to be either chunked or truncated. The model outputs floats, formatted as a list of floats, with one-dimensional parameters. Each value corresponds to a raw logit. Users can choose to apply a Sigmoid activation function to the logits to convert them into probabilities during model usage. The model requires transformers version 4.47.1. The model is compatible with NVIDIA Ampere, NVIDIA Hopper, and NVIDIA Lovelace hardware microarchitectures, and it supports Linux as the operating system. The Llama Nemotron Reranking 1B model is part of the NeMo Retriever collection of NIM and is identified by the short name llama-nemotron-rerank-1b-v2. The model was trained on 800k samples from public datasets. The evaluation datasets are based on three TextQA datasets, the TechQA dataset, MIRACL, MLDR, and MLQA multilingual retrieval datasets, which are all public datasets. The sizes range between 10,000s up to 5M depending on the dataset. The pipeline llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2 + llama-nemotron-rerank-1b-v2 provides high BEIR+TechQA accuracy with multilingual and crosslingual support. The llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2 ranking model is 3.5x smaller than the nv-rerankqa-mistral-4b-v3 model. The pipeline achieves an average Recall@5 of 73.64% on the NQ, HotpotQA, FiQA, and TechQA datasets. The model achieves an average Recall@5 of 65.80% on the MIRACL multilingual datasets. The model achieves an average Recall@5 of 86.83% on the MLQA dataset with different languages. The model achieves an average Recall@5 of 70.69% on the MLDR dataset. The model’s max sequence length is 8192. Therefore, the longer text inputs should be truncated. The model does not always guarantee to retrieve the correct passage(s) for a given query. Use of this model is governed by the license. Additional information is available. NVIDIA believes Trustworthy AI is a shared responsibility and has established policies and practices to enable development for a wide array of AI applications. Developers should work with their supporting model team to ensure this model meets requirements for the relevant industry and use case and addresses unforeseen product misuse. For more detailed information on ethical considerations for this model, please see the Explainability, Bias, Safety, and Privacy sections. Please report security vulnerabilities or NVIDIA AI Concerns. The model is intended for passage ranking for question and answer retrieval. It is designed for use by generative AI creators working with conversational AI models—users who want to build a multilingual question and answer application over a large text corpus, leveraging the latest dense retrieval technologies. The model transforms the tokenized input text into a dense vector representation. The model's output is an array of float numbers (dense vector representation for the input text). The model's performance is measured in terms of accuracy, throughput, and latency. The model does not always guarantee to retrieve the correct passage(s) for a given query. The model’s max sequence length is 8192. Therefore, the longer text inputs should be truncated. The model has not been tested to deliver comparable outcomes for any adversely impacted groups. The model has been verified to meet prescribed NVIDIA quality standards. The model does not generate or reverse engineer personally-identifiable information (PII). No personal data was used to create this model. The dataset is reviewed before every release. There is no mechanism in place to honor data subject right of access or deletion of personal data. Personal data was not collected for the development of this model by NVIDIA. Data labeling (annotation, metadata) complies with privacy laws. Data is not compliant with data subject requests for data correction or removal, if such a request was made. The model application is text reranking for retrieval. There is no physical safety impact. Use of this model is governed by the license. Additional information is available. The Principle of least privilege (PoLP) is applied limiting access for dataset generation and model development. Restrictions enforce dataset access during training, and dataset license constraints are adhered to.

instruction: What is the main idea of this news article about recent AI advancements?
description: Gemma 3n is a family of lightweight, state-of-the-art open models developed by Google, built using the same research and technology as the Gemini models. These models are designed for efficient execution on low-resource devices and are capable of handling multimodal input, including text, image, video, and audio, to generate text outputs. They are available in pre-trained and instruction-tuned variants and were trained on data in over 140 spoken languages. Gemma 3n models use selective parameter activation technology to reduce resource requirements, allowing them to operate at an effective size of 2B and 4B parameters, which is lower than the total number of parameters they contain. The raw parameter count of the model is 6B, but the architecture design allows the model to be run with a memory footprint comparable to a traditional 2B model by offloading low-utilization matrices from the accelerator. The models use a MatFormer architecture that allows nesting sub-models within them. One sub-model is provided in this model repository, or users can access a spectrum of custom-sized models. The models support a total input context of 32K tokens and can generate outputs up to 32K tokens, subtracting the request input tokens. Inputs include text strings, images normalized to 256x256, 512x512, or 768x768 resolution and encoded to 256 tokens each, and audio data encoded to 6.25 tokens per second from a single channel. Outputs include generated text in response to the input, such as an answer to a question, analysis of image content, or a summary of a document. The models were trained on a dataset totaling approximately 11 trillion tokens, with a knowledge cutoff date of June 2024. The training data includes web documents, code, mathematics, images, and audio. Rigorous data filtering methods were applied, including CSAM filtering, sensitive data filtering, and additional methods based on content quality and safety. The models were trained using TPUv4p, TPUv5p, and TPUv5e hardware, leveraging JAX and ML Pathways software for efficient training. Evaluation results show the models perform well across various benchmarks, including reasoning, factuality, multilingual tasks, STEM and code, and additional benchmarks. The models were evaluated for safety and ethics, including child safety, content safety, and representational harms. They showed safe levels of performance relative to previous Gemma models, with minimal policy violations and significant improvements in high severity violations. The models have certain limitations, including training data biases, context and task complexity, language ambiguity, factual accuracy, and common sense reasoning. Ethical considerations include bias and fairness, misinformation and misuse, transparency and accountability, and privacy violations. The models are intended for a wide range of applications, including content creation, research, and education. They provide high-performance open generative model implementations designed for responsible AI development.

instruction: Design an illustration of a space explorer standing on an alien planet with unusual flora and a double sun in the sky.
description: HunyuanImage-3.0 is a native multimodal model that unifies multimodal understanding and generation within an autoregressive framework. It is designed for image generation and supports tasks such as text-to-image generation, prompt self-rewrite, and multi-turn interaction. The model employs a unified autoregressive architecture, moving beyond traditional DiT-based designs, and is the largest open-source image generation Mixture of Experts (MoE) model to date, with 64 experts and a total of 80 billion parameters, of which 13 billion are activated per token. It demonstrates superior image generation performance, achieving a balance between semantic accuracy and visual quality through advanced dataset curation and reinforcement learning post-training. The model also exhibits intelligent world-knowledge reasoning, allowing it to interpret user intent and elaborate on sparse prompts to produce detailed and contextually appropriate visual outputs. Evaluation results include structured semantic alignment scores (SSAE) and human evaluations (GSB), with HunyuanImage-3.0 showing strong performance in both. The model requires a Linux operating system, NVIDIA GPU with CUDA support, and at least 3×80GB GPU memory for optimal performance. It is available for download and use with specific dependencies and installation instructions provided. Performance optimizations such as FlashAttention and FlashInfer can significantly speed up inference. The model is currently available in a pretrain checkpoint and an Instruct checkpoint, with the latter supporting prompt rewriting and reasoning. Users are advised to consult the official documentation for best practices in prompt writing and system configuration.

instruction: Generate a cinematic shot of a stormy sea with lightning flashing across the sky.
description: Self Forcing is a method for training autoregressive video diffusion models by simulating the inference process during training, using autoregressive rollout with KV caching. This approach addresses the train-test distribution mismatch and allows for real-time, streaming video generation on a single RTX 4090 while maintaining the quality of state-of-the-art diffusion models. The model is designed to generate high-quality video content and supports both GUI and CLI-based inference. It is trained using a data-free algorithm, meaning no video data is required, and instead uses text prompts and an ODE-initialized checkpoint. The model can be trained using multiple GPUs, with a training run of 600 iterations completing in under 2 hours using 64 H100 GPUs. Performance can be optimized by enabling `torch.compile` or using FP8 Linear layers, though these may affect quality. The model works best with long, detailed prompts, and it is recommended to use third-party LLMs like GPT-4o to extend prompts before input. The model supports chunk-wise autoregressive generation and is evaluated using prompts from the MovieGenVideoBench dataset. The codebase is based on open-source implementations from other repositories. The model is available on HuggingFace and requires an Nvidia GPU with at least 24 GB memory, a Linux operating system, and 64 GB RAM for optimal performance.

instruction: How does this mole compare to typical benign skin growths?
description: DermLIP is a vision-language model for dermatology, trained on the Derm1M dataset, which is the largest dermatological image-text corpus to date. The model variant PanDerm-base-w-PubMed-256 uses domain-specific pretraining to achieve superior performance compared to other DermLIP variants. It is a pretrained vision-language model with a CLIP-style architecture. The vision encoder is PanDerm-base, and the text encoder is PubMedBert-256. The model processes images at a resolution of 224×224 pixels. It was trained on 403,563 skin image-text pairs from the Derm1M dataset, which includes both dermoscopic and clinical images. The training objective was to minimize image-text contrastive loss, and the model was trained using an Nvidia H200 GPU with approximately 90GB of memory over a period of about 9.5 hours. The intended uses of the model include zero-shot classification, few-shot learning, cross-modal retrieval, and concept annotation or explanation. The model is distributed under the cc-by-nc-nd-4.0 license.

instruction: I need to adjust the font and color of the text in a logo. Can you help?
description: Qwen-Image-Edit-2509 is the monthly iteration of Qwen-Image-Edit, introduced in September. It is designed to support image editing tasks, with improvements over the previous version released in August. The model supports multi-image editing, allowing combinations such as 'person + person,' 'person + product,' and 'person + scene,' with optimal performance observed when using 1 to 3 input images. For single-image inputs, it significantly improves editing consistency, particularly in preserving facial identity for person editing, product identity for product editing, and text content, fonts, colors, and materials for text editing. The model also includes native support for ControlNet, enabling the use of depth maps, edge maps, keypoint maps, and other related features. It is built upon the Qwen-Image-Edit architecture and further trained via image concatenation to enable multi-image editing. The model is licensed under Apache 2.0.

instruction: Extract the key details from this conversation where a caller is reporting a case of child exploitation.
description: This model is a fine-tuned version of the google/flan-t5-base model, specifically optimized for summarizing child helpline case call transcripts. It has been trained on domain-specific data to better understand and summarize conversations involving child protection issues. The model uses the T5ForConditionalGeneration architecture and operates in English with 248 million parameters. It is designed for the task of text summarization within the domain of child protection and helpline conversations. The model has been specialized to understand child protection terminology, helpline conversation patterns, sensitive case reporting protocols, and legal and procedural references specific to child welfare. It demonstrates enhanced contextual understanding of child welfare scenarios, including child labor, forced marriage, and abuse cases. The model generates concise, actionable summaries that capture key information such as caller identity and location, the nature of the concern or issue, and action items and referrals provided. It is trained to appropriately summarize sensitive child protection cases while maintaining essential details. The model has a maximum source length of 1024 tokens and a maximum target length of 256 tokens. It was trained for 3 epochs with a learning rate of 3e-5 and a batch size of 4. It uses beam search with 4 beams, a length penalty of 2.0, and a no repeat n-gram size of 2. The model is intended for use by child protection organizations, helpline services, social workers, and researchers for automated summarization of case calls, quick generation of case summaries, efficient case documentation, and analysis of child protection case patterns and trends. The model has limitations, including domain specificity, language restriction to English, a context window limited to 1024 input tokens, and the need for human review of outputs for critical cases. Ethical considerations include the handling of sensitive information, the need for professional review of outputs, the maintenance of privacy and confidentiality protocols, and the model's role as an assistant rather than a replacement for human judgment in child protection cases. The model shows significant improvements over the base FLAN-T5 model, with 50-84% improvement across ROUGE-1, ROUGE-2, and ROUGE-L metrics, 94% accuracy in using child protection terminology, 91% success rate in identifying key case details, 87% accuracy in identifying referrals and follow-up actions, and a 4.4/5 overall quality rating from child protection professionals. Performance highlights include better identification of key information in child protection contexts, more structured and actionable summaries, proper use of child protection terminology and procedures, more reliable output format across different case types, and summaries that meet standards for official case documentation. Evaluation results include a Rouge1 score of 0.5804, a Rouge2 score of 0.3623, a Rougel score of 0.5325, a train loss of 0.8403, and a val loss of 0.8031.

instruction: What is the most prominent feature in this image, and how does it relate to the surrounding elements?
description: Qwen3-VL is the most powerful vision-language model in the Qwen series. It provides comprehensive upgrades in text understanding and generation, visual perception and reasoning, context length, spatial and video dynamics comprehension, and agent interaction capabilities. The model is available in Dense and MoE architectures, with Instruct and reasoning-enhanced Thinking editions for flexible deployment. Key enhancements include the ability to operate PC and mobile GUIs by recognizing elements, understanding functions, invoking tools, and completing tasks. It can generate Draw.io, HTML, CSS, and JS from images and videos. The model supports advanced spatial perception, including judging object positions, viewpoints, and occlusions, with capabilities for 2D and 3D grounding. It has a native 256K context length, expandable to 1M, enabling it to handle books and hours-long video with full recall and second-level indexing. The model excels in enhanced multimodal reasoning, particularly in STEM and Math, with causal analysis and logical, evidence-based answers. It features upgraded visual recognition with broader, higher-quality pretraining that can recognize a wide range of subjects, including celebrities, anime, products, landmarks, flora, and fauna. The model supports expanded OCR in 32 languages, with robust performance in low light, blur, and tilt, and improved handling of rare or ancient characters and jargon. It provides text understanding comparable to pure LLMs, with seamless text–vision fusion for lossless, unified comprehension. Architectural updates include Interleaved-MRoPE for full-frequency allocation over time, width, and height, DeepStack for fusing multi-level ViT features to capture fine-grained details and sharpen image–text alignment, and Text–Timestamp Alignment for precise, timestamp-grounded event localization to strengthen video temporal modeling. This is the weight repository for the Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Thinking variant.

instruction: Could you create a voice sample that matches the tone of this provided audio clip?
description: OuteTTS Version 1.0 is a compact, high-performance text-to-speech (TTS) model designed for speech synthesis and voice cloning. It supports a wide range of languages and features automatic word alignment, native multilingual text processing, and enhanced metadata integration for improved synthesis quality. The model uses a DAC audio encoder with two codebooks for high-fidelity audio reconstruction and includes special tokens for audio codebooks. It supports one-shot voice cloning with approximately 10 seconds of reference audio and can align raw text at the word level, even for languages without clear word boundaries. The model is trained on approximately 60,000 hours of audio and supports a maximum context window of 8,192 tokens. It is optimized for use with a speaker reference to maintain vocal characteristics, emotion, and accent. For optimal performance, a temperature of 0.4 is recommended, along with a repetition penalty of 1.1 applied to a 64-token recent window. The model is built on a base Llama3.2 architecture and has undergone continued pre-training and fine-tuning. It is intended for ethical use in applications that enhance accessibility, creativity, and communication, with prohibitions on impersonation, misleading content, harassment, and unauthorized voice cloning. The model is available through the outetts library, which automatically sets up necessary samplers and patches for all backends.

instruction: How do I batch generate variations of an industrial product based on a single input image?
description: Qwen-Image-Edit-2511 is an enhanced version of Qwen-Image-Edit-2509, featuring multiple improvements, including notably better consistency. Key enhancements include mitigating image drift, improved character consistency, integrated LoRA capabilities, enhanced industrial design generation, and strengthened geometric reasoning ability. The model is designed to perform imaginative edits based on an input portrait while preserving the identity and visual characteristics of the subject. It further enhances consistency in multi-person group photos, enabling high-fidelity fusion of two separate person images into a coherent group shot. Qwen-Image-Edit-2511 integrates selected popular LoRAs directly into the base model, unlocking their effects without extra tuning. For example, it supports realistic lighting control and generating new viewpoints directly with the base model. The model is also optimized for industrial design applications, such as batch industrial product design and material replacement for industrial components. Additionally, it introduces stronger geometric reasoning capability, such as directly generating auxiliary construction lines for design or annotation purposes. Qwen-Image-Edit-2511 is licensed under Apache 2.0.

instruction: Is there a handwritten signature visible in this photo of a signed agreement?
description: This model is a Conditional-DETR with a ResNet-50 backbone, fine-tuned for the task of detecting handwritten signatures in document images. It was selected as the best-performing model among various object detection architectures based on its highest mAP@0.5 score of 93.65% in a comprehensive evaluation. The model was trained on a dataset consisting of 2,819 document images, with 1,980 images used for training (70%), 420 for validation (15%), and 419 for testing (15%). The dataset was processed to a resolution of 640x640 pixels in COCO JSON format. During the training process, multiple models were evaluated for precision, recall, and inference time, with Conditional-DETR ResNet-50 achieving the best mAP@0.5 score. Hyperparameter tuning was performed on the YOLOv8s model using Optuna, with parameters including dropout, learning rate, box and class loss coefficients, and optimizer selection. After tuning, the best model achieved a precision of 94.74%, recall of 89.72%, mAP@50 of 94.50%, and mAP@50-95 of 67.35%. Inference times were 171.56 ms on CPU (ONNX Runtime) and 7.657 ms on GPU (TensorRT). The model can be used for inference via the Hugging Face Transformers library, and it supports post-processing for object detection results. A live demo is available for real-time inference, and the model can be deployed using the NVIDIA Triton Inference Server. The model was developed using Python 3.10.12, PyTorch 2.5.1, and related libraries, and trained on a Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 instance with an NVIDIA Tesla T4 GPU. The model weights, code, and training materials are licensed under the Apache 2.0 license.

instruction: Design a modern Scandinavian bedroom with white walls, a sleek bed frame, and a minimalist aesthetic.
description: FLUX.1-Layout-ControlNet is a ControlNet conditioned by a semantic image. It is capable of generating a 2D image based on a text description while following the semantic image layout. This ControlNet is applicable to FLUX.1 [dev] and is leveraged within the SpatialGen framework for 3D scene synthesis. It supports generating images of bedrooms with specific styles, such as serene Chinese-inspired, elegant European-style, whimsical cartoon, and futuristic cyberpunk. The model is designed to be used with the diffusers Python library, requiring installation or upgrade of diffusers and peft. It can be used with the FluxControlNetPipeline, which loads the model and processes input images and prompts to generate output images. The model uses a control image to guide the layout of the generated image and allows for customization of parameters such as conditioning scale, number of inference steps, guidance scale, and image dimensions. The model is trained to follow the layout of the control image while incorporating the stylistic and descriptive elements from the text prompt.

instruction: Can you generate a visual companion for this voice message that moves as it speaks?
description: SoulX-FlashTalk is a real-time, infinite streaming model for audio-driven avatars, developed using self-correcting bidirectional distillation. It is designed to generate continuous, real-time animations of avatars based on audio input, supporting a wide range of styles including portrait, animal animation, and fast-paced rap. The model is built with a 14B parameter architecture and is optimized for deployment on high-end GPU systems, such as 8xH800 or higher, to achieve real-time inference speeds. A 4-GPU version is planned for consumer-grade GPUs like the 4090. The model relies on components such as the `chinese-wav2vec2-base` for audio processing. Evaluation results and specific performance metrics are not detailed in the provided text. The model is available for download via Hugging Face, and inference requires a Conda environment with specific PyTorch and dependency versions. An online demo is coming soon. The model is based on foundational models and techniques from external sources, and the authors acknowledge these contributions. The model is described as being under active development, with a technical report and project page already released.

instruction: What are the most significant legal concepts mentioned in this ruling?
description: GEM-RoBERTa HQ Legal is a RoBERTa-base model pre-trained from scratch on a strategically curated 21GB corpus of Greek legal, parliamentary, and governmental text. The model employs a quality-based data repetition strategy, where higher-quality legal sources are repeated multiple times during training to enhance the model's understanding of premium legal terminology and concepts. This model was trained as part of a research project and has been optimized for downstream tasks such as Named Entity Recognition (NER), Text Classification, and Question Answering within the legal field. The RoBERTa architecture provides enhanced performance through dynamic masking and removal of the Next Sentence Prediction (NSP) task, focusing entirely on Masked Language Modeling (MLM). The model was pre-trained on a comprehensive corpus of Greek text compiled from various legal and governmental sources, with a quality-based data repetition strategy that increases exposure to higher-quality legal content. The original 16.75GB corpus was expanded to 21.12GB through strategic repetition. The quality-based repetition strategy enhances the model's exposure to premium legal terminology from the Raptarchis Legal Dictionary (4x repetition), high-quality judicial reasoning from Supreme Court reports (3x repetition), and EU legal concepts from Eur-Lex content (2x repetition). This approach ensures the model develops a stronger understanding of sophisticated legal language while maintaining exposure to the broader legal corpus. The model uses the RoBERTa-base architecture with a hidden size of 768, 12 attention heads, 12 hidden layers, and approximately 125 million parameters. The model was trained using a custom ByteLevelBPE tokenizer with a vocabulary of 50,264 tokens optimized for Greek legal terminology. The data was processed into fixed-size chunks of 512 tokens, respecting document boundaries to ensure contextual coherence. Higher-quality sources were strategically repeated during the data preparation phase. The model was pre-trained from scratch for 150,000 steps on 8x NVIDIA A100 40GB GPUs, using BFloat16 (bf16) mixed-precision for stability and speed. The training took approximately 81 hours and 39 minutes to complete. The key hyperparameters used were a learning rate of 1.5e-4 (0.00015) with a linear warmup of 9,000 steps, an effective batch size of 2,688 (per_device_train_batch_size: 48, gradient_accumulation_steps: 7), the AdamW optimizer with standard parameters, a weight decay of 0.01, a max sequence length of 512, a max of 150,000 steps, 9,000 warmup steps, an MLM probability of 0.15, and a max gradient norm of 1.0. The model achieved a final training loss of 0.617 and a final evaluation loss of 0.573035. The training infrastructure consisted of 8x NVIDIA A100 40GB GPUs, with a total of 150,000 training steps and a total training time of 66 hours 39 minutes. The train/validation split was 95%/5%, and the effective training data was 21.12GB, incorporating the quality-based repetition strategy.

instruction: Convert the sentence 'Each student has a unique ID' into a formal logical expression.
description: This model is a fully fine-tuned version of the T5 architecture, developed by Vossel et al. at Osnabrück University and funded by the Deutsche Forschungsgemeinschaft (DFG, German Research Foundation) under grant number 456666331. It is designed to translate natural language (NL) statements into First-Order Logic (FOL) representations. The model is an encoder-decoder sequence-to-sequence model, supporting English and FOL. It was fine-tuned from the google/t5-base model and is released under the Apache 2.0 License. The model is intended for use in automated semantic parsing, explainable AI systems requiring symbolic reasoning, research in neurosymbolic AI, and integration into pipelines for natural language inference, question answering, or knowledge base population. It is not designed for general natural language generation and may struggle with ambiguous, highly figurative, or out-of-domain input. Outputs should be validated for correctness before use, especially in critical applications. The model should not be used as the sole basis for decisions in critical areas without expert review. It is specialized for English NL and FOL and may not generalize to other languages or logics. Human-in-the-loop workflows are recommended for sensitive tasks. The model is intended for research and prototyping, not for standalone critical systems. The model was fine-tuned on two datasets: WillowNLtoFOL, which contains over 16,000 NL-FOL pairs and is licensed under CC BY-NC-ND 4.0, and MALLS-v0, which contains 34,000 NL-FOL pairs generated by GPT-4 and syntactically checked, licensed under Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0, subject to OpenAI terms. The training procedure involved full fine-tuning from the google/t5-base model with prompt-based instruction tuning, single-GPU training in float32 precision, and preprocessing that replaced FOL quantifiers (e.g., ∀) with tokens like FORALL. The maximum input/output sequence length was 250 tokens. Training hyperparameters included a batch size of 8 per device, a learning rate of 0.001, 12 epochs, the AdamW optimizer with an Adam epsilon of 1e-8, a linear warmup scheduler with 500 steps, no gradient accumulation, a weight decay of 0.01, no LoRA (full fine-tuning), a task type of SEQ_2_SEQ_LM, early stopping with a patience of 4 epochs, an evaluation strategy per epoch, a save strategy per epoch, a save total limit of 5 checkpoints, and best model selection based on eval_loss.

instruction: Is it possible to make the sky in this photo look more dramatic and cloudy?
description: Qwen-Image-Edit is an image editing model built upon the 20B Qwen-Image model. It extends Qwen-Image's text rendering capabilities to support image editing tasks, enabling precise text editing. The model simultaneously processes input images through Qwen2.5-VL for visual semantic control and the VAE Encoder for visual appearance control, allowing both semantic and appearance editing. Semantic editing includes tasks such as IP creation, object rotation, and style transfer, where the overall pixel content may change while maintaining semantic consistency. Appearance editing involves low-level modifications such as adding, removing, or modifying specific elements while keeping other regions unchanged. Qwen-Image-Edit supports bilingual (Chinese and English) text editing, allowing direct addition, deletion, and modification of text in images while preserving the original font, size, and style. Evaluations on multiple public benchmarks show that Qwen-Image-Edit achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance in image editing tasks, making it a powerful foundation model for the field. The model is capable of tasks such as novel view synthesis, object rotation, style transfer, and detailed appearance modifications, including the addition of signboards with reflections and the removal of fine hair strands. It also supports chained editing approaches for progressive correction of errors in generated content, such as calligraphy artworks. Qwen-Image-Edit is licensed under Apache 2.0.

instruction: Could you generate a 3D model for the following protein fragment: 'AGQKTEAELKAL'?
description: ESM-2 is a state-of-the-art protein model trained on a masked language modelling objective. It predicts protein structures from amino acid sequences, leveraging a transformer-based architecture for accurate 3D modeling. It is suitable for fine-tuning on a wide range of tasks that take protein sequences as input. This version of the ESM-2 model is optimized with NVIDIA's library. It is based on the original ESM-2 model from Facebook Research, and (within numerical precision) has identical weights and outputs. Slight numerical differences may be observed between the original model and the optimized version. The model is ready for commercial and non-commercial use. It is not owned or developed by NVIDIA but has been developed and built to a third-party's requirements for this application and use case. ESM-2 is licensed under a specified license. It is available for global deployment and is intended for use in protein structure prediction, specifically predicting 3D protein structures from amino acid sequences. The model was released on Hugging Face on 07/29/2025. The model architecture is based on a transformer with the ESM-2 network architecture. It has 1.5 x 10^10 model parameters. The input type is text in the form of protein sequences, formatted as a string, with a one-dimensional input. The input consists of a protein sequence represented as a string of canonical amino acids, with a maximum length of 1022. Longer sequences are automatically truncated to this length. The output type is embeddings at the amino acid and sequence level, formatted as a vector with one-dimensional parameters. The output is a numeric vector with floating-point values corresponding to an embedding for each amino acid in the input protein sequence, with a maximum output length of 1022 embeddings. The model is designed and/or optimized to run on NVIDIA GPU-accelerated systems, leveraging NVIDIA’s hardware (e.g., GPU cores) and software frameworks (e.g., CUDA libraries) to achieve faster training and inference times compared to CPU-only solutions. The model integrates with the Hugging Face Transformers runtime engine and is compatible with NVIDIA Ampere, Blackwell, and Hopper hardware microarchitectures. It is supported on Linux operating systems. Integration of foundation and fine-tuned models into AI systems requires additional testing using use-case-specific data to ensure safe and effective deployment. Iterative testing and validation at both unit and system levels are essential to mitigate risks, meet technical and functional requirements, and ensure compliance with safety and ethical standards before deployment. Several ESM-2 checkpoints are available with varying sizes. Larger checkpoints have better accuracy but require more memory and time to train. The training datasets include UniRef90 and UniRef50 clusters generated from UniRef100 seed sequences using the MMseqs2 algorithm. The data modality is text in the form of protein sequences, with a training data size ranging from 1 billion to 10 trillion tokens. The data collection method is human, and no labeling method is used. The evaluation datasets include data collected from sequences of protein structures about to be released by the Protein Data Bank (PDB) and from CASP14 targets. The benchmark scores for these datasets are 0.72 and 0.55, respectively. The data collection method for both is human, and no labeling method is used. The model is accelerated using the Hugging Face Transformers engine and has been tested on A100, H100, H200, and GB200 hardware. NVIDIA believes Trustworthy AI is a shared responsibility and has established policies and practices to enable development for a wide array of AI applications. When downloaded or used in accordance with the terms of service, developers should work with their internal model team to ensure the model meets requirements for the relevant industry and use case and addresses unforeseen product misuse. Users are responsible for ensuring the physical properties of model-generated molecules are appropriately evaluated and comply with applicable safety regulations and ethical standards.

instruction: How can I re-rank retrieved items to focus on a specific category, like technical specifications or user guides?
description: Contextual AI Reranker v2 1B is a text reranking model designed to re-rank retrieved documents based on custom instructions, such as prioritizing recent information. It is the first instruction-following reranker capable of handling retrieval conflicts and ranking documents according to user-defined criteria. The model achieves state-of-the-art performance on the BEIR benchmark and is positioned on the cost/performance Pareto frontier across several dimensions, including instruction following, question answering, multilinguality (supporting 100+ languages), product search and recommendation, and real-world use cases. It is a 1 billion parameter model with a context length of up to 32,000 tokens. The model is intended for use when re-ranking retrieved documents with custom instructions, handling conflicting information in retrieval results, prioritizing documents by recency or other criteria, supporting multilingual search, and processing long contexts. The model is available for use with either the vLLM or Transformers frameworks, with specific implementation details provided for each. It is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution Non Commercial Share Alike 4.0 license. For questions or issues, users are directed to open an issue on the model repository. If used, the model should be cited with the provided BibTeX entry.

instruction: How can I get a sense of how far away the objects in this image are?
description: The DPT (Dense Prediction Transformer) model with DINOv2 backbone is a depth estimation model proposed by Oquab et al. It is designed to demonstrate that using the DPT framework with DINOv2 as the backbone results in a powerful depth estimator. The model supports depth estimation tasks and is intended for use in applications requiring accurate depth prediction from images. The architecture combines the DPT framework with the DINOv2 backbone, which is known for learning robust visual features without supervision. The model can be used with the Transformers library by loading the appropriate image processor and model from the 'facebook/dpt-dinov2-base-kitti' repository. The model processes images, generates depth predictions, and allows for visualization of the depth output. The model is based on research published in the paper titled 'DINOv2: Learning Robust Visual Features without Supervision' available on arXiv with the identifier 2304.07193.

instruction: Can you provide a detailed breakdown of how to calculate the derivative of this function?
description: INTELLECT-3 is a 106B (A12B) parameter Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) reasoning model post-trained using supervised fine-tuning (SFT) followed by large-scale reinforcement learning (RL). Training was conducted using environments built with a specific library, and all training and evaluation environments are available on the Environments Hub. The model, training frameworks, and environments are open-sourced under fully-permissive licenses, including MIT and Apache 2.0. INTELLECT-3 achieves best-in-class performance on math, coding, and reasoning benchmarks, including 98.1 on MATH-500, 90.8 on AIME24, 88.0 on AIME25, 69.3 on LCB, 74.4 on GPQA, 14.6 on HLE, and 81.9 on MMLU-Pro. The BF16 version of the model can be served on two H200 GPUs using the vLLM framework with specific configuration parameters, while the FP8 version can be served on a single H200 GPU with similar settings. The model is designed to support reasoning tasks and is part of the Prime Intellect Platform. For more information, refer to the technical report and blog.

instruction: What are some common use cases for this kind of system in research?
description: Gemma 3n is a family of lightweight, state-of-the-art open models developed by Google, built using the same research and technology as the Gemini models. These models are designed for efficient execution on low-resource devices and are capable of handling multimodal input, including text, image, video, and audio, to generate text outputs. They are available in pre-trained and instruction-tuned variants and were trained on data in over 140 spoken languages. Gemma 3n models use selective parameter activation technology to reduce resource requirements, allowing them to operate at an effective size of 2B and 4B parameters, which is lower than the total number of parameters they contain. The models are available in two sizes, with the raw parameter count of this model being 6B, but the architecture design allows it to be run with a memory footprint comparable to a traditional 2B model by offloading low-utilization matrices from the accelerator. They use a MatFormer architecture that allows nesting sub-models within the model. The models support a total input context of 32K tokens and can generate outputs up to 32K tokens, subtracting the request input tokens. The training dataset includes a wide variety of sources totaling approximately 11 trillion tokens, with a knowledge cutoff date of June 2024. The dataset includes web documents, code, mathematics, images, and audio. Rigorous data filtering methods were applied, including CSAM filtering, sensitive data filtering, and additional methods based on content quality and safety. The models were trained using TPUv4p, TPUv5p, and TPUv5e hardware, leveraging JAX and ML Pathways software for efficient training. Evaluation results show that the models perform well across a range of benchmarks, including reasoning, factuality, multilingual tasks, STEM and code, and additional benchmarks. The models were evaluated for safety and ethics, including child safety, content safety, and representational harms. They showed safe levels of performance relative to previous Gemma models, with minimal policy violations and significant improvements in high severity violations. The models have certain limitations, including potential biases in training data, challenges with complex tasks, difficulties with language ambiguity and nuance, and potential factual inaccuracies. Ethical considerations include the risk of perpetuating biases, generating harmful content, misuse for malicious purposes, and privacy violations. The models are intended for a wide range of applications, including content creation, research, and education. They provide high-performance open generative model implementations designed for responsible AI development.

instruction: I found a clip online claiming to show a celebrity. Could you verify if it's real?
description: This model is a powerful deepfake video detection system that combines a ResNext50 network for spatial feature extraction with an LSTM network for temporal analysis. It is designed to classify video authenticity by identifying visual artifacts and temporal inconsistencies. The model achieves 87% accuracy on internal test datasets and is built using PyTorch. It is trained on a custom dataset of real and deepfake videos and provides a binary classification (real/fake) along with a confidence score. The architecture is optimized for video clips of 20 frames and performs best on human face-focused content. Its performance may be affected by video quality, such as heavy compression or low resolution. The model is intended for ethical use in combating misinformation and verifying media authenticity. It is integrated with the Hugging Face pipeline for easy inference and is available as a Docker-ready deployment with a Hugging Face Space.

instruction: I need to locate a document that includes a screenshot of the error message I received. Can you search for it?
description: ColModernVBERT is the late-interaction version of ModernVBERT that is fine-tuned for visual document retrieval tasks and is the most performant model on this task. The ModernVBERT suite consists of compact 250M-parameter vision-language encoders that achieve state-of-the-art performance in this size class, matching the performance of models up to 10 times larger. ColModernVBERT is designed to support visual document retrieval tasks and is part of a family of models that includes BiModernVBERT, ModernVBERT-embed, and the base ModernVBERT model. The base ModernVBERT model is trained using a masked language modeling objective for modality alignment, while ModernVBERT-embed is further trained using contrastive learning without document specialization. BiModernVBERT is a bi-encoder version fine-tuned for visual document retrieval. ColModernVBERT matches the performance of models nearly 10 times larger on visual document benchmarks and provides an interesting inference speed on CPU compared to models of similar performance. The model is released under the MIT license. If used in academic work, it should be cited with the provided citation information.
